pi_items.source: Consistent definitions across setup_nx_witness.sh (line 199), setup_task.sh (line 30), and verifier.py (lines 48-54). Camera names are initialized in environment setup and verified by task verifier.

Network Optix official documentation confirms port 7001 is the default port for Nx Witness Server. Verified via https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/community/posts/16800938628247. Installation script confirms this configuration (install_nx_witness.sh, setup_nx_witness.sh).

Network Optix official documentation and user manuals confirm 'admin' is the standard administrator username for Nx Witness systems. Verified via https://support.networkoptix.com/hc/en-us/community/posts/4404148686615 and multiple Network Optix support articles.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses Nx Witness VMS (real software from Network Optix installed from official repositories, versions 5.1.5.39242 or 6.0.1.39873). The expected camera renames are: Parking Lot Camera→HQ-EXT-PKG-01, Entrance Camera→HQ-1F-ENT-01, Server Room Camera→HQ-B1-SVR-01, Lobby Camera→HQ-1F-LBY-01, Loading Dock Camera→HQ-EXT-LDK-01. The system is configured with API at https://localhost:7001 and admin user 'admin'. Cameras and video streams are synthetic (generated by testcamera and FFmpeg test patterns). The naming convention BLDG-FLOOR-ZONE-SEQ is a custom task-specific convention, not a built-in Nx Witness standard.
